summary: Added configurable API throttling, strict JSON request validation, and schedule stale-write protection, then covered the changes with SQL injection, rate-limit, and concurrency regression tests.
reason: Security task 12 required application-wide abuse controls, safer request handling, and schedule race-condition protection with real regression tests instead of documentation-only fixes.
risk: MEDIUM — the change adds new request rejection paths (415, 413, 429, 409) and process-local rate limiting. Existing successful flows were kept intact and covered by integration tests.
tests: go test ./tests/integration/modules/auth; go test ./tests/integration/modules/schedules ./tests/integration/modules/teams ./tests/integration/modules/players ./tests/integration/modules/reports; go test ./internal/...
notes: A batched go test invocation that included the auth package hit a Windows/rootless-Docker provider issue in testcontainers-go, but the auth package passed when run on its own. The current rate limiter is in-memory per process; the runbook records shared-store or edge limiting as a follow-up for multi-instance deployments.
